title: Oedera calycina subsp. lanceolata
what_description: Oedera calycina subsp. lanceolata is a botanical subspecies of Oedera calycina, belonging to the family Asteraceae. It is a shrubby perennial herb with a woody base, growing up to 50 cm in height, and has lanceolate or elliptic leaves that are 2-5 cm in length, often covered with a greyish or white powdery coating. The leaves are sessile, alternate, and have a linear or cuneate base. The inflorescence is a capitulum, comprising 10-20 florets with ligulate corolla and 5-merous disc florets, yellow in color, and produces achenes 2-3 mm in length. The subspecies is endemic to southern Africa, specifically in the Western Cape and Eastern Cape provinces.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Oedera calycina subsp. lanceolata are inflammation and skin irritations, particularly contact dermatitis, which are effectively reduced due to its anti-inflammatory properties and essential oils that soothe and calm the skin. Fungal infections such as athlete's foot can be treated with its antifungal properties, while wound healing is facilitated by its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties that accelerate tissue repair. Diarrhea is alleviated by its anti-inflammatory properties that reduce intestinal inflammation and promote the repair of the gut lining. The plant's essential oils also have a cooling effect on insect bites, wounds, and burns, making it an effective treatment for these conditions. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to reduce the risk of gastrointestinal issues, such as dyspepsia, by protecting the gut from oxidative stress and promoting digestive health.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Oedera calycina subsp. lanceolata are attributed to its antimicrobial activity, which is primarily due to the presence of flavonoids and volatile oils in this plant, capable of inhibiting the growth of various pathogens. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of Oedera calycina subsp. lanceolata contribute to its ability to neutralize free radicals and protect against oxidative stress, which is often associated with chronic diseases. Its anti-inflammatory properties are also noteworthy, as they may help alleviate symptoms of inflammation and reduce the risk of related complications. Furthermore, the antifungal properties of this plant make it a potential candidate for treating fungal infections, which could be attributed to the presence of saponins and other bioactive compounds. The analgesic properties of Oedera calycina subsp. lanceolata may also provide relief from pain, possibly due to the interactions between its bioactive compounds and the body's pain pathways.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Oedera calycina subsp. lanceolata are flavonoids, saponins, phenolic acids, tannins, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Flavonoids, particularly kaempferol and quercetin, exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, contributing to its therapeutic effects. Saponins, such as oleanolic acid and ursolic acid, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, while phenolic acids, including caffeic and ferulic acid, demonstrate antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. Tannins, including gallic and ellagic acid, have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, and glycosides, like aucubin, possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making Oedera calycina subsp. lanceolata a valuable medicinal plant. The combination of these constituents in Oedera calycina subsp. lanceolata is responsible for its broad range of medicinal properties, including wound healing, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory activities.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Oedera calycina subsp. lanceolata are derived from its leaves and flowers, which are used to create an infusion by steeping the plant material in hot water to extract its bioactive compounds. The infusion can be consumed as a tea, providing a mild diuretic and anti-inflammatory effect. A tincture can be prepared by soaking the plant material in a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin, allowing for a concentrated extract that can be used in various applications. A salve or ointment can be created by infusing the plant material in a carrier oil, such as olive or coconut oil, to produce a topical preparation for wound healing and skin issues. These preparations are typically used for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been documented in various studies and traditional uses.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Oedera calycina subsp. lanceolata improperly include developing skin irritation due to allergic contact dermatitis caused by the plant's sap or oils. This is because Oedera calycina subsp. lanceolata contains sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds that can cause skin irritation in some individuals. Additionally, improper use of the plant may lead to experiencing nausea and abdominal pain, as well as experiencing allergic reactions such as hives and itching, due to the plant's toxic properties. Suffering from diarrhea is also possible as a result of the plant's irritant effects on the gastrointestinal tract. These side effects are typically associated with ingestion, topical application, or prolonged exposure to the plant's sap or oils.
